LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DIAMOND HEIGHTS VILLAGE ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL PHYSICAL EXAM ON PLAINTIFF - PLAINTIFF SANCHEZ IS ORDERED TO APPEAR FOR A PHYSICAL EXAMINATION AT THE EMBASSY SUITES, SAN FRANCISCO AIRPORT, 250 GATEWAY BLVD., SOUTH SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94080. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE TIME AND DATE OF THE EXAMINATION. THE EXAMINATION SHALL OCCUR BY OCTOBER 15, 2013. THE ENTIRE EXAMINATION SHALL LAST NOT LONGER THAN TWO HOURS AND WILL BE CONDUCTED BY ANDREW SAXON, M.D. THE EXAMINATION WILL BE A GENERAL PHYSICAL EXAMINATION AND SPIROMETRY TO BE DONE IN A PAINLESS, PASSIVE MANNER. A MORE FOCUSED DETAILED EXAMINATION MAY BE CONDUCTED FOLLOWING THE GENERAL EXAMINATION TO BETTER IDENTIFY THE SOURCE OF ANY SPECIFIC PROBLEMS WHICH MAY INCLUDE: A SPIROMETRY AFTER THE INHALATION OF A BRONCHODILATOR IF THE AT REST BREATHING SHOWS EVIDENCE OF AN OBSTRUCTIVE ABNORMALITY; THIS TEST INVOLVES THE INHALATION OF TWO BREATHS OF MEDICATION, WHICH IS DESIGNED TO RELIEVE THE OBSTRUCTION FOLLOWED BY PERFORMANCE OF THE BREATHING TEST IN 20 MINUTES. THE PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO COOPERATE DURING THE EXAMINATION SO THAT IT MAY BE COMPLETED WITHIN THE TWO HOUR LIMITATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WILLIAM RALPH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS TIMELY. (SEE CCP 437C(A); 12A.) DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN BY PRODUCING PRIMA FACIE EVIDENCE THAT HE DID NOT ACT WITH MALICE, OPPRESSION, OR FRAUD AS CONTEMPLATED UNDER CIV. CODE. 3294. DEFENDANT PRODUCES EVIDENCE THAT HE REMAINED IN COMMUNICATION WITH PLAINTIFFS AS THE LEAKS WERE OCCURRING, AND THAT HE SPENT OVER $1000 AND HUNDREDS OF HOURS ATTEMPTING RESOLVE THE PLUMBING ISSUES. (DECLARATION OF RALPH IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, 8-10) ALSO, DEFENDANT HAS PRODUCED EVIDENCE THAT THERE IS NO WAY HE COULD HAVE PLACED THE BROWN BAG OF LIQUID IN THE SPACE BETWEEN HIS UNIT'S FLOOR AND PLAINTIFFS' UNIT'S CEILING. (DECLARATION OF HUNTER IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, 7.) CONSEQUENTLY, THE BURDEN SHIFTS TO PLAINTIFFS. PLAINTIFFS POINT TO SEVERAL PIECES OF EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN FRAUDULENT BEHAVIOR. PLAINTIFF POINTS TO DEFENDANT'S STATEMENTS DENYING THAT WATER HAD MIGRATED FROM HIS UNIT TO PLAINTIFFS' APARTMENT, AND THAT HE HAD NEVER RECEIVED A LETTER FROM HIS INSURANCE COMPANY ON OCTOBER 21, 2012 REQUESTING THAT HE TAKE RESPONSIBILITY FOR THE LEAK. (SEE EVIDENCE CITED IN PLAINTIFFS' SEPARATE STATEMENT OF FACTS IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION ("PSSF"), NOS. 18, 21.) THE EVIDENCE SHOWS DEFENDANT LATER ADMITS THESE STATEMENTS ARE INCORRECT. THIS IS SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN BY CLEAR AND CONVINCING EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT ACTED FRAUDULENTLY REGARDING HIS ROLE IN PLAINTIFFS' INJURY. (CIV. CODE 3294.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)

Defendant Diamond Heights Village Association'S Motion To Compel Physical Exam On Plaintiff - Plaintiff Sanchez is ordered to appear for a physical examination at the Embassy Suites, San Francisco Airport, 250 Gateway Blvd., South San Francisco, CA 94080. The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding the time and date of the examination. The examination shall occur by October 15, 2013. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. S. CLINTON WOODS OF BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 433-4800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. MICHAEL K. BUDRA OF THE BIERNAT LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. (650) 689-5160 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT WILLIAM RALPH;
ATTY. WAKAKO T. URITANI OF LORBER, GREENFIELD & POLITO, LLP, TEL. NO. 986-0688 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT DHVA.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant WILLIAM RALPH's motion for summary adjudication is denied. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Defendant's motion is timely. (See CCP 437c(a); 12a.) Defendant shifted the burden by producing prima facie evidence that he did not act with malice, oppression, or fraud as contemplated under Civ. Code. 3294. Defendant produces evidence that he remained in communication with Plaintiffs as the leaks were occurring, and that he spent over $1000 and hundreds of hours attempting resolve the plumbing issues. (Declaration of Ralph in Support of Motion for Summary Adjudication, 8-10) Also, Defendant has produced evidence that there is no way he could have placed the brown bag of liquid in the space between his unit's floor and Plaintiffs' unit's ceiling. (Declaration of Hunter in Support of Motion for Summary Adjudication, 7.) Consequently, the burden shifts to Plaintiffs. Plaintiffs point to several pieces of evidence showing that create a triable issue of fact as to whether Defendant engaged in fraudulent behavior. Plaintiff points to Defendant's statements denying that water had migrated from his unit to Plaintiffs' apartment, and that he had never received a letter from his insurance company on October 21, 2012 requesting that he take responsibility for the leak. (See Evidence cited in Plaintiffs' Separate Statement of Facts in Opposition to Motion ("PSSF"), Nos. 18, 21.) The evidence shows Defendant later admits these statements are incorrect. This is sufficient evidence to create a triable issue of material fact as to whether Plaintiff has shown by clear and convincing evidence that Defendant acted fraudulently regarding his role in Plaintiffs' injury. (Civ. Code 3294.)
ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
